facts.statement: Binary search finds a target in a sorted array.
questions: What does binary search do?
How does binary search work?
What is the purpose of binary search?

facts.statement: Binary search has O(log n) time complexity.
questions: What is the time complexity of binary search?
How fast is binary search?
What is the Big O notation for binary search?

facts.statement: Binary search requires the input array to be sorted.
questions: What are the requirements for binary search?
Does binary search work on unsorted arrays?
What type of array does binary search need?

facts.statement: Binary search returns the index of the target element when found.
questions: What does binary search return when it finds the target?
What is the return value of binary search on success?
How does binary search indicate a match?

facts.statement: Binary search returns -1 when the target is not found.
questions: What does binary search return when the target is not found?
What happens if binary search cannot find the element?
How does binary search indicate failure?

facts.statement: Binary search calculates the midpoint using (lo + hi) >> 1.
questions: How does binary search calculate the midpoint?
What formula is used to find the middle element in binary search?
How is the mid index computed in binary search?

facts.statement: Binary search moves to the right half when the middle element is less than the target.
questions: When does binary search search the right half?
How does binary search decide to move right?
What happens when arr[mid] is less than the target?

facts.statement: Binary search moves to the left half when the middle element is greater than the target.
questions: When does binary search search the left half?
How does binary search decide to move left?
What happens when arr[mid] is greater than the target?
